title: Composer @mentions, ask_user / QuestionHub, and experimental surface gating

thesis.text: This layer establishes the new composer and structured-question foundation: users can mention Conversations and Task Sessions through rich input, while both the Main Chat Agent and Workspace Agent can pause for ask_user, accept answers, and resume execution.

claim.text: Hide unfinished Agents and Mini Apps surfaces; add the @mention hub, Conversation pills, and structured QuestionHub.

evidence.name: Surface gate

claim.text: The model emits AskUserInput, the shared schema validates it, UIMessage or Task Session replay persists it, and the user's answers return as a real tool result.

claim.text: Main Chat resumes through assistant-ui addResult; Task Sessions resume from a needs-input checkpoint and persisted replay.

evidence.name: Main Chat continuation

claim.text: Existing Space and Conversation access guards remain in force; the PR does not introduce a new cross-Space permission path.

evidence.name: Access boundary

claim.text: Main Chat repairs abandoned calls with a skipped result; Task Session answer failures and concurrent submissions do not receive equivalent protection.

evidence.name: Abandoned call repair
Partial validation then update

claim.text: The 378 passing tests cover schemas, runner replay, backend APIs, and pure state helpers, but they do not prove that preview reaches the UI or that answer claiming is concurrency-safe.

evidence.name: Replay proof
Narrow Task Session mapper
Partial validation then update

claim.text: Three product features and three vendored Aura syncs are bundled into a 4,835-line review unit, creating a broad review surface.

evidence.name: Independent behavior line

claim.text: The shared schema already carries preview, but two handwritten parsers introduce narrower shadow contracts and cause the feature to drift.

evidence.name: Narrow Main Chat mapper
Narrow Task Session mapper

claim.text: Option previews disappear in both renderer paths: the Main Chat and Task Session parsers copy only label and description, so QuestionHub never receives preview.

evidence.name: Narrow Main Chat mapper
Narrow Task Session mapper

claim.text: The answer trust boundary checks only known keys: it does not verify completeness, duplicate keys, single-select cardinality, or that selected values belong to the original options; an unconditional update also does not provide an atomic concurrent claim.

evidence.name: Partial validation then update

claim.text: Workspace Agent question suspension and replay have backend integration coverage.

evidence.name: Replay proof

claim.text: The synthetic result for an abandoned Main Chat question has a focused pure-function regression test.

evidence.name: Abandoned call regression proof

claim.text: It remains unproven that a real tool part containing preview reaches QuestionHub intact.

evidence.name: Narrow Main Chat mapper
Narrow Task Session mapper

claim.text: API behavior for invalid, missing, duplicate, or concurrent answers remains unproven.

evidence.name: Partial validation then update

summary.text: Consolidate renderer parsing on the shared schema or one shared mapper so preview is preserved; validate the complete question set at the Task Session answer boundary and use an atomic claim. The gating and replay direction can otherwise remain.
